title: RN Habit Tracker
description: A premium productivity ecosystem featuring a Pomodoro focus timer, real-time task management, and automatic DND mode for deep work.
overview: A comprehensive mobile application designed to enhance productivity by integrating task management seamlessly with a Pomodoro timer.
problem: Users often struggle to balance focused work sessions with organized task lists. Most existing solutions separate these into different apps, leading to context switching.
solution: Built a unified mobile dashboard combining a custom Pomodoro timer directly linked with a local database task queue, featuring automatic OS-level notifications and an integrated work-session lock to minimize notification disruptions.
approach: Developed using React Native and Expo with Zustand for atomic state management, storing task sequences in an optimized SQLite local database to ensure zero-latency offline performance.
learnings: Leveraged native notification listener hooks and background threads, realizing the importance of state synchronization between JS threads and native alarm schedulers to handle timing constraints reliably.

title: Adaptive Noise Cancellation
description: High-performance embedded system implemented on STM32 to actively filter ambient noise using adaptive signal processing algorithms.
overview: Hardware-level active noise cancellation utilizing advanced DSP techniques on a low-power STM32 microcontroller.
problem: Software-based noise cancellation often introduces latency. A hardware-level approach is needed for real-time, zero-latency processing.
solution: Designed a low-latency, hardware-based digital active filter that samples ambient noise, computes its exact anti-noise phase shift in real-time, and outputs the canceling waveform with under 1ms of delay.
approach: Programmed in Embedded C on STM32 microcontrollers. Utilized DMA (Direct Memory Access) pipelines connected directly to high-speed ADC and DAC units to process audio frames without CPU intervention.
learnings: Gained deep insights into numerical stability of digital signal filters, learning that fixed-point arithmetic optimization is critical when deploying active filtering models on low-power hardware.

title: AI Intelligence Suite
description: Next-generation AI application exploring the boundaries of LLM integration for personalized productivity and automation.
overview: A web-based platform that leverages OpenAI's APIs to provide a suite of intelligent tools tailored for personal and professional automation.
problem: Generic AI chatbots lack context and specialized tools for complex, multi-step workflows. Users need a centralized hub for custom AI tasks.
solution: Created a next-generation automation dashboard featuring semantic context memory, multi-stage LLM chains, and specialized workspace tool agents that automate repetitive productivity workflows.
approach: Constructed using Next.js and TypeScript, using LangChain for managing prompt state and semantic vectors, integrated with an optimized PostgreSQL vector database for long-term user memory.
learnings: Discovered that raw prompting is highly volatile; implementing vector-based few-shot context inject structures vastly improved structural consistency and reduced model hallucinations across multi-step flows.

title: Smart Helmet OS
description: Comprehensive hardware and software integration for a motorcycle smart helmet, featuring real-time telemetry and safety alerts.
overview: An integrated embedded operating system for a smart motorcycle helmet, managing sensors, Bluetooth communication, and a heads-up display.
problem: Motorcyclists need to access navigation and safety alerts without taking their eyes off the road. Current solutions are either bulky or lack integration.
solution: Developed a real-time hardware operating system that processes heads-up display telemetry, Bluetooth audio feeds, and IMU-based impact detection alerts into a safe, integrated biker interface.
approach: Implemented using Embedded C with an RTOS scheduler for predictable thread execution, interfacing directly over SPI/I2C protocols to handle accelerometer and HUD display displays.
learnings: Learned to manage stringent power budgets and structural memory limits on microcontroller boards, discovering that hardware interrupt safety is paramount for reliable crash-detection routines.
